title: Requests Request
description: JSON Schema describing the parameters accepted by the Python Requests library's main request() function and convenience methods (get, post, put, patch, delete, head, options).

params.description: Dictionary, list of tuples, or bytes to send in the query string.

data.description: Dictionary, list of tuples, bytes, or file-like object to send in the request body (form-encoded).

json.description: A JSON-serializable Python object to send as application/json in the request body.

headers.description: Dictionary of HTTP headers to send with the request.

cookies.description: Dictionary or CookieJar of cookies to send with the request.

files.description: Dictionary of name to file-like objects for multipart form upload.

auth.description: Auth tuple (username, password) for Basic Auth, or an auth callable for custom auth schemes.

timeout.description: Seconds to wait for the server to respond. Float for single timeout; [connect_timeout, read_timeout] tuple for separate timeouts.

allow_redirects.description: Whether to follow redirects. Defaults to True for GET/POST/etc., False for HEAD.

proxies.description: Dictionary mapping protocol schemes to proxy URLs (e.g., {'https': 'http://proxy:8080'}).

verify.description: Boolean to control TLS certificate verification, or path string to a CA bundle. Defaults to True.

stream.description: If False, response content is downloaded immediately. If True, streaming mode is used.

cert.description: Path to SSL client certificate .pem file, or a ['cert', 'key'] tuple.
